primary_official_source.name: EUR-Lex -- Official Journal of the European Union

obligations.source_quote: High-risk AI systems shall be designed and developed in such a way, including with appropriate human-machine interface tools, that they can be effectively overseen by natural persons during the period in which they are in use.

decomposed_atoms.description: System must be designed for effective human oversight
decomposed_atoms.requirement: Systems SHALL be designed and developed to enable effective oversight by natural persons

decomposed_atoms.description: Appropriate human-machine interface tools must be included
decomposed_atoms.requirement: Systems SHALL include appropriate human-machine interface tools

decomposed_atoms.description: Oversight must be possible during use period
decomposed_atoms.requirement: Oversight SHALL be possible during the period in which the system is in use

automation_assessment.detection_method: Check for human-in-the-loop patterns: approval flows, review queues, manual review gates, human-machine interface components
automation_assessment.rationale: Can detect oversight patterns but cannot assess effectiveness

obligations.source_quote: Human oversight shall aim to prevent or minimise the risks to health, safety or fundamental rights that may emerge when a high-risk AI system is used in accordance with its intended purpose or under conditions of reasonably foreseeable misuse, in particular where such risks persist despite the application of other requirements set out in this Section.

decomposed_atoms.description: Oversight must aim to prevent/minimise risks
decomposed_atoms.requirement: Human oversight SHALL aim to prevent or minimise risks to health, safety, or fundamental rights

decomposed_atoms.description: Must cover intended use and foreseeable misuse
decomposed_atoms.requirement: Oversight SHALL address risks from both intended purpose and foreseeable misuse

automation_assessment.detection_method: Check for risk mitigation documentation linked to human oversight procedures
automation_assessment.human_judgment_needed: Whether oversight measures adequately address identified risks
automation_assessment.rationale: Risk prevention effectiveness requires domain expertise

obligations.source_quote: The oversight measures shall be commensurate with the risks, level of autonomy and context of use of the high-risk AI system, and shall be ensured through either one or both of the following types of measures:

decomposed_atoms.description: Oversight must be proportionate to risks
decomposed_atoms.requirement: Oversight measures SHALL be commensurate with risks, autonomy level, and context of use

decomposed_atoms.description: Art.14(3)(a): measures built into the system by provider
decomposed_atoms.requirement: Measures SHALL be identified and built into the system when technically feasible

decomposed_atoms.description: Art.14(3)(b): measures for deployer to implement
decomposed_atoms.requirement: Measures SHALL be identified for deployer implementation

automation_assessment.detection_method: Check for override mechanisms (kill switch, manual override, emergency stop), deployer-facing documentation of oversight procedures
automation_assessment.human_judgment_needed: Whether measures are proportionate to the specific risks and autonomy level
automation_assessment.rationale: Can detect override mechanisms but cannot assess proportionality

obligations.source_quote: For the purpose of implementing paragraphs 1, 2 and 3, the high-risk AI system shall be provided to the deployer in such a way that natural persons to whom human oversight is assigned are enabled, as appropriate and proportionate:

decomposed_atoms.description: Art.14(4)(a): understand system capabilities and limitations
decomposed_atoms.requirement: Oversight persons SHALL be enabled to understand relevant capacities and limitations, monitor operation, detect anomalies
Oversight persons SHALL remain aware of automation bias tendency
Oversight persons SHALL be enabled to correctly interpret the system's output
Oversight persons SHALL be enabled to disregard, override or reverse the output

decomposed_atoms.description: Art.14(4)(e): ability to interrupt system (stop button)
decomposed_atoms.requirement: Oversight persons SHALL be enabled to intervene or interrupt through a stop button or similar

automation_assessment.detection_method: Check for: monitoring dashboards, system documentation describing capabilities/limitations, anomaly detection code, performance monitoring, model cards, override/stop mechanisms, confidence scores, automation bias warnings
automation_assessment.human_judgment_needed: Whether all 5 capabilities (a-e) are adequately provided
automation_assessment.rationale: Can detect oversight UI patterns and override mechanisms but cannot assess adequacy

obligations.source_quote: For high-risk AI systems referred to in point 1(a) of Annex III, the measures referred to in paragraph 3 of this Article shall be such as to ensure that, in addition, no action or decision is taken by the deployer on the basis of the identification resulting from the system unless that identification has been separately verified and confirmed by at least two natural persons with the necessary competence, training and authority.
obligations.scope_limitation: Only applies to biometric identification systems (Annex III, point 1(a))

decomposed_atoms.description: Biometric identification must be verified by at least 2 persons
decomposed_atoms.requirement: No action SHALL be taken unless identification is separately verified by at least two natural persons

decomposed_atoms.description: Verifiers must have competence, training and authority
decomposed_atoms.requirement: The natural persons SHALL have necessary competence, training and authority

automation_assessment.detection_method: Check for dual-approval workflows, multi-person verification gates, role-based access requiring 2+ approvals
automation_assessment.human_judgment_needed: Whether the dual verification is genuinely independent and persons are qualified
automation_assessment.rationale: Can detect multi-approval patterns but cannot assess person qualifications

obligations.source_quote: The requirement for a separate verification by at least two natural persons shall not apply to high-risk AI systems used for the purposes of law enforcement, migration, border control or asylum, where Union or national law considers the application of this requirement to be disproportionate.
obligations.scope_limitation: Only applies to law enforcement, migration, border control or asylum systems where national/Union law deems dual verification disproportionate
obligations.note: Exemption from ART14-OBL-5 dual verification requirement. Applies when: (1) system is used for law enforcement, migration, border control, or asylum, AND (2) Union or national law considers dual verification disproportionate. NOT a general exemption.

decomposed_atoms.description: Dual verification exempt for qualifying law enforcement use
decomposed_atoms.requirement: Dual verification requirement SHALL NOT apply to law enforcement/migration/border/asylum systems where national/Union law deems it disproportionate

automation_assessment.detection_method: N/A — legal exemption requiring case-by-case assessment
automation_assessment.human_judgment_needed: Whether system is used for law enforcement/migration/border/asylum AND applicable law deems dual verification disproportionate
automation_assessment.rationale: Proportionality assessment under applicable Union/national law requires legal expertise

scope_notes.general: ART14-OBL-1 through ART14-OBL-4 -- apply to ALL high-risk AI systems
scope_notes.conditional: ART14-OBL-5 -- only biometric identification systems (Annex III, point 1(a))
